title: Daily Briefing — Jun 15
headline: Tech surges but rotation signals and elevated sector risk mean this rally needs scrutiny.
body: Right, so let's look at what the flows are telling us. We’re seeing a broad bounce across US equities — SPY up 2.0% to 756.33, QQQ up 3.2% to 744.14, and XLK (tech) leading the charge with a 3.8% pop at 191.84. VIX is creeping higher (17.68 above the 20-day MA), signalling volatility is on the rise even as folks chase the green. Fear & Greed is sitting at 34 — low, but not extreme panic territory.

Sector rotation data is mental — materials (XLB) and utilities (XLU) are quietly improving, while energy (XLE), financials (XLF), staples (XLP), and healthcare (XLV) all flag high risk and remain laggards. The leadership is concentrated in tech, but sectors that should act as the canary (XLF, XLV, XLP) are sick. Breadth is narrow, with momentum clustering around a handful of mega-caps (XLK, AMD, DELL, ARM), while cyclicals and defensive sectors are either lagging or only starting to improve.

Contrarian warning: most tech tickers — AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, GOOGL, META, AMZN — are still below their daily 20 MA, so don’t chase; this isn’t confirmation, it’s rebound noise. Energy is getting hammered: USO -4.6%, CL=F -5.4%. Financials (XLF) are up, but still below my mean reversion weekly trigger. Watch for retail piling in after today's squeeze — the rally is suspect until we see genuine dark pool accumulation and a weekly close above 20 MA across leaders. Abundance mindset: don’t chase, look for the next rotational play.
callout_body:
SPY: 745.93 (SMA20) — watch for daily close
QQQ: 723.26 (SMA20) — mean reversion zone
XLK: 184.81 (SMA20) — sector momentum
AAPL: 303.74 (SMA20) — resistance overhead
HYG: 80.14 — any breakdown = instant risk reduction
XLF: 53.92 (watch weekly MA for health)
USO: 135.80 (SMA20), 119.68 price — high risk energy trade
action_items: Do NOT chase tech breakout unless daily closes reclaim 20 MA (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, GOOGL, META, AMZN)
Watch for rotational setups in XLB, XLU, XLI — improving momentum and lower risk
closing_quote: If bonds freak out, everything breaks. Patience, react, don’t predict.
